subject: Ecology
title: How Roots Find Nutrients
description: A 60-second lesson on How Roots Find Nutrients.

hook.text: Plant roots don't just sit there. They actively hunt for nutrients in the soil.

buildup.text: Root tips sense chemical gradients and grow toward patches richer in phosphorus or nitrogen.

discovery.text: Root hairs increase surface area by hundreds of times, letting roots mine tiny soil pores.

twist.text: Plants also exude sugars from roots to attract helpful bacteria and fungi to their zone.

climax.text: The rhizosphere—soil within millimeters of a root—is a hotspot of microbial activity.

punchline.text: Roots don't just absorb. They recruit.
